input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install the .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (discovers latest stable automatically)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --accept-source-agreements

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name/version
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (pulls Android, Windows, iOS stubs, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target dependency)

Windows-target MAUI apps require the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which come with VS or VS Build Tools:

```powershell
# Option A: Full Visual Studio (recommended)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Option B: Build Tools only (CI / lightweight)
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components via the VS Installer CLI:
# (run from an elevated prompt)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload bundles an Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and verify paths:

```powershell
# Discover where the workload placed the Android SDK
dotnet android sdk info  2>$null
# Fallback: check the default path
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"
Test-Path $androidSdk

# If you need the standalone SDK via cmdline-tools:
winget search Google.AndroidStudio   # optional, for the emulator GUI
# Or install command-line tools directly:
$cmdLineUrl = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-latest.zip"
Invoke-WebRequest $cmdLineUrl -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools"

# Set env vars
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk

# Use sdkmanager to discover and install components
$sdkmanager = Get-ChildItem "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools" -Recurse -Filter "sdkmanager.bat" |
              Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty FullName

# List available platforms/build-tools (discover versions dynamically)
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -Last 5
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Select-Object -Last 5

# Install what MAUI needs (pick latest from the list above)
& $sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses   # answer 'y' to each
```

## 5. Set Up an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "system-images.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install an image + create AVD
& $sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" "emulator"

$avdmanager = Get-ChildItem "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools" -Recurse -Filter "avdmanager.bat" |
              Select-Object -First 1 -ExpandProperty FullName

& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Launch it
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI
```

## 6. Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for deploying unpackaged/dev WinUI apps
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"
# Then toggle "Developer Mode" ON in the UI that opens
```

## 7. Create and Run a Test Project

```powershell
# List MAUI templates to confirm availability
dotnet new list maui

# Create a project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Deploy to running emulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Troubleshoot / Validate

```powershell
# Confirm all workloads healthy
dotnet workload repair

# Check for known issues
dotnet new maui-blazor --dry-run   # verifies templates resolve

# Ensure Java is available (MAUI bundles OpenJDK, but verify)
dotnet android sdk info
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0-*` with your actual SDK version (`dotnet --version` tells you).
- The `--list` flags on `sdkmanager` and `dotnet workload search` let you **discover versions at runtime** instead of hardcoding.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for fast Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
